meta.title: Greenways video knowledge pointers
meta.description: Lightweight summaries + takeaways per video so Cheryce can explain what a clip is about without loading full transcripts.

items.title: BUILDING with RAMMED EARTH
items.summary: A quick tour of rammed-earth construction and why it performs well: thick thermal mass, durability, and low embodied impact compared with conventional materials in the right context.
takeaways: Thermal mass can stabilise indoor temperatures (comfort + reduced HVAC swings).
Material choice affects both operational energy and embodied carbon — it’s a whole-life conversation.
Best used as inspiration; feasibility depends on local codes, climate, and builder experience.

items.title: An Artist's Home by a Stream — Tauhai Villa
items.summary: A sustainability-in-action architecture vignette focused on site-sensitive design — how layout, daylight, and materials shape comfort and energy use before you add technology.
takeaways: Passive design (orientation, shading, airflow) often matters before new equipment spend.
Daylight and layout choices can reduce lighting loads and improve comfort.
Treat as inspiration, then translate into a practical retrofit plan for your own building.
